title: Express 5 Routing, Path Syntax, Parameters, and Dispatch · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for express 5 routing, path syntax, parameters, and dispatch and demands the smallest safe Express 5 route grammar and dispatch table with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for Express 5 route grammar and dispatch table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Author Express 5.2 string paths with named wildcards, braces, reserved characters, and supported path-to-regexp syntax.
Derive dispatch from registration order, method, path, router mounting, next-route behavior, and literal-versus-parameter overlap.
Treat decoded string or wildcard-array parameters as untrusted input and validate identifier grammar, length, canonical form, and existence separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Express5 Path Syntax · changed case · Quiz Express5 Path Syntax
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 5455 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to preserve Express 5 route order and named wildcard-array evidence; record only bounded redacted diagnostic fields; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Author Express 5.2 string paths with named wildcards, braces, reserved characters, and supported path-to-regexp syntax.
